channel.title: Microblog on Lev Lazinskiy
channel.description: Recent content in Microblog on Lev Lazinskiy

item.title: Bsky F83ee0bc
item.description: Bsky def feels like 2007 era Twitter right now.

item.title: Plan9 8dd98542
item.description:
TIL that plan9 is still being developed and they are having an international conference in Paris next year.

https://iwp9.org/

item.title: Existential 34b8c81c
item.description:
Had a nice boost of existential dread this morning by reading “A Time for Compassion” from Rene Coignard where he compares life on this planet to a hospice.

To me, birth seems like the final stage of a terminal illness, inevitably followed by death.

Deep.

https://renecoignard.com/a-time-for-compassion/

item.title: Signal F3a11fd8
item.description:
Redownloaded signal after listening to the CEO Meredith Whittaker on the Kara Swisher podcast. I don’t remember why I stopped using it, but excited to give it another go.

https://megaphone.link/VMP1340571392

item.title: St Frank C3a2c383
item.description:
A few weeks ago my favorite local Coffee Shop (St. Frank on Folsom) remembered my order when I walked in and I was on top of the world becuase its hard to become a “local” in downtown SF.

Today they gave me a free cappucino. Our relationship is escalating and I’m madly in love. 🥰

item.title: Kindle Free A5eb9e61
item.description:
How to make an Amazon-free Kindle, via Rodrigo Ghedin’s blog. I had no idea this was possible. Very interesting.

https://notes.ghed.in/posts/2024/amazon-free-kindle/

@manualdousuario@mastodon.social

item.title: Rss Dbe5acc7
item.description: I added the little RSS easter egg I found in @kev@fosstodon.org feed to my own. :) Thanks for the inspiration!

item.title: Cliche C8a05338
item.description: Ok fine, you can have a cliche in your essay. But if I read two cliches in your opening paragraph, I’m closing the tab.

item.title: Bsky
item.description: Updated my Bluesky Dagger Module to support links, #hashtags, and mentions using the official Typescript SDK instead of the Python one.

item.title: Bsky
item.description: Bluesky seems to be getting a lot of buzz today!

item.title: Clutter
item.description:
The joy of clutter. A look into the other side of Japanese minimalism.

Tsuzuki dismissed the West’s obsession with Japanese minimalism as ‘some Japanophile’s dream’ in the introduction to the English translation of Tokyo: A Certain Style (1999).

https://aeon.co/essays/the-life-changing-magic-of-japanese-clutter

item.title: Arc
item.description: When are we going to get Arc for Linux Desktop? 👉👈

item.title: Gogen
item.description:
A guide on go code generator magic via Evil Martians.

https://evilmartians.com/chronicles/a-taste-of-go-code-generator-magic-a-quick-guide-to-getting-started

item.title: Opp
item.description:
You down with OPP*? Yeah you know me.

*other peoples principles

item.title: Principles
item.description:
I really love reading other people’s principles, starting with Ray Dalio. This one really stood out to me from Nabeel Qureshi, attributed to John Collison.

“The world is a museum of passion projects.”

https://nabeelqu.co/principles

item.title: Cameron
item.description:
Via @brad_frost@mastodon.social, this is truly amazing. An explosion of nostalgia:

https://bradfrost.com/blog/link/camerons-world/

item.title: Walk and Talk
item.description:
Derek Sivers wrote about this walk and talk that he did for seven days in Thailand over 100km with 8 other people. It sounds amazing, something I’d really like to do some day.

https://sive.rs/wt

item.title: Gnome
item.description:
GNOME infrastrucfture is moving to AWS

https://www.dragonsreach.it/2024/11/16/gnome-infrastructure-migration-to-aws/

item.title: Sahil
item.description:
Happy Birthday to @sahil@toots.sahilister.in ! In his reflection he mentioned:

It’s hard for an individual to learn, grow and understand the world in a silo.

So true.

https://blog.sahilister.in/2024/10/25-a-quarter-of-a-century-later/

item.title: Weird
item.description: Still seeing some weird stuff in my posse module but overall I am happy with progress. Possible I’ll never lose another toot!

item.title: Secondattempt
item.description:
I made a lot of progress tonight on my goals for using hugo + Dagger to POSSE my way to success. Using a jenky technique that compares the existing to generated xml feed as a lazy database. If the system works you will see this post somewhere outside of my site.

https://levlaz.org/joining-the-posse-club/

item.title: Posse
item.description: This is my first post made with the posse system!
